name: First Prize Chocolate Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Ladies' Home Journal Magazine - February 19
Lots of chocolate chips but only a small amount of dry ingredients were used in this award-winning recipe. To achieve just the right texture, chill the cake overnight before serving.
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 9-inch springform pan and wrap the outside of the pan securely with foil.
2. In a saucepan over very low heat, melt chocolate with water and instant coffee powder, stirring occasionally until melted. Allow mixture to cool to room temperature.
3. In a mixer bowl, beat butter until creamy. Add sugar and beat until light and fluffy for about 5 minutes. Beat in egg yolks one at a time, ensuring well combined after each addition. At low speed, gradually add flour and stir in the cooled chocolate mixture.
4. In a clean bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually increase speed and continue beating until stiff but not dry. Gently fold this into the chocolate mixture.
5. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 60 to 65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted about 1 inch from the side comes out clean (the top may crack). Allow to cool in the pan on a rack before removing the sides of the pan. Dust lightly with confectioners' sugar over the top. Makes 14 to 16 servings.
